question: best open source vector database
ground_truth: Qdrant, Weaviate, and Milvus are popular open-source vector databases. Qdrant is known for performance and Rust-based implementation. Weaviate supports hybrid search natively. Chroma is a lightweight option for local development.

question: free framework for building AI agents
ground_truth: LangChain and LlamaIndex are widely used free frameworks for building AI agents. CrewAI is an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework. AutoGen by Microsoft enables conversational multi-agent workflows.

question: tool for automating browser workflows with AI
ground_truth: Playwright and Puppeteer are browser automation tools. AgentGPT and AutoGPT can browse the web autonomously. Browser-use and Multion are AI-native browser automation tools.

question: open source LLM inference server
ground_truth: Ollama is popular for running LLMs locally. vLLM is a high-throughput inference engine. LM Studio provides a UI for local LLMs. Text Generation Inference (TGI) by Hugging Face is production-grade.

question: tool for evaluating RAG pipelines
ground_truth: RAGAS is the standard framework for RAG evaluation. TruLens and DeepEval are alternatives. LangSmith provides tracing and evaluation for LangChain-based systems.

question: AI coding assistant for VS Code
ground_truth: GitHub Copilot is the most popular AI coding assistant. Cursor is an AI-native IDE. Codeium and Continue are free alternatives. Tabnine offers privacy-focused code completion.

question: workflow automation with LLMs
ground_truth: n8n and Zapier support LLM-powered automations. LangChain provides chains and agents for workflow automation. Flowise is a low-code LangChain builder. Rivet and Langflow offer visual LLM workflow builders.

question: embedding model for semantic search
ground_truth: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small is widely used. BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 is a strong open-source alternative. Cohere Embed and Voyage AI offer high-quality embeddings. Sentence-transformers library provides many pretrained models.

question: tool for fine-tuning LLMs
ground_truth: Unsloth and Axolotl are popular open-source fine-tuning frameworks. LoRA and QLoRA techniques enable efficient fine-tuning. Hugging Face TRL supports RLHF-based fine-tuning. Replicate provides hosted fine-tuning.

question: AI image generation API
ground_truth: Stability AI provides the Stable Diffusion API. OpenAI DALL-E 3 is available via API. Replicate hosts many image generation models. Fal.ai offers fast image generation inference.

question: open source RAG framework
ground_truth: LlamaIndex is purpose-built for RAG applications. LangChain provides RAG primitives and chains. Haystack by deepset is a production RAG framework. txtai and FastRAG are lightweight alternatives.

question: tool for monitoring LLM applications in production
ground_truth: LangSmith provides tracing and monitoring for LangChain. Langfuse is an open-source LLM observability platform. Phoenix by Arize supports LLM tracing. Helicone is an LLM proxy with analytics.

question: freemium vector database with cloud hosting
ground_truth: Pinecone offers a free starter tier with cloud hosting. Weaviate Cloud and Qdrant Cloud have free tiers. Chroma Cloud and LanceDB Cloud are emerging options.

question: multi-agent AI framework
ground_truth: CrewAI enables role-based multi-agent collaboration. AutoGen by Microsoft supports conversational agents. LangGraph provides stateful multi-agent workflows. MetaGPT simulates a software development team.

question: no-code LLM application builder
ground_truth: Flowise and Langflow are visual no-code LLM builders. Botpress supports no-code chatbot development. Stack AI and Relevance AI are no-code AI app platforms.

question: tool for document parsing and extraction
ground_truth: Unstructured.io parses PDFs, Word, and HTML documents. LlamaParse is specialized for complex PDF extraction. Docling and Marker are open-source document parsers. Adobe PDF Extract API handles enterprise documents.

question: open source alternative to ChatGPT
ground_truth: Ollama with Llama 3 or Mistral provides local LLM chat. Open WebUI is a self-hosted ChatGPT-style interface. Jan.ai is a desktop LLM client. LibreChat supports multiple LLM providers.

question: AI tool for generating code from natural language
ground_truth: GitHub Copilot and Cursor generate code from prompts. Cody by Sourcegraph understands codebases. GPT-4 and Claude via API are used for code generation. Replit AI integrates generation into a cloud IDE.

question: tool for building chatbots
ground_truth: LangChain with memory supports chatbot development. Botpress and Rasa are chatbot frameworks. Voiceflow builds conversational AI interfaces. Chainlit provides a chat UI for LLM apps.

question: vector search with metadata filtering
ground_truth: Pinecone supports metadata filtering with $eq and $in operators. Qdrant and Weaviate support payload filtering. pgvector with PostgreSQL enables vector search with SQL filters.

question: LLM router or orchestration tool
ground_truth: LiteLLM is a unified API for routing between LLM providers. PortKey and OpenRouter act as LLM gateways. LangChain's router chains select strategies per query.

question: AI tool for summarizing documents
ground_truth: LlamaIndex and LangChain support document summarization chains. Quivr and Notion AI summarize uploaded documents. Claude's 200K context window handles long documents natively.

question: paid AI research assistant
ground_truth: Perplexity AI Pro is a paid AI search and research tool. Elicit specializes in academic research. Consensus searches scientific papers with AI. You.com Research Mode is another option.

question: tool for prompt engineering and testing
ground_truth: PromptLayer tracks and versions prompts. Promptfoo tests prompts across models. LangSmith evaluates prompt performance. Weights & Biases Prompts provides prompt versioning.

question: data pipeline tool for AI applications
ground_truth: Airflow and Prefect orchestrate data pipelines. Dagster is built for data-intensive workflows. Bytewax enables streaming data pipelines in Python. Unstructured handles AI-focused ETL.

question: free AI tool for writing and content generation
ground_truth: ChatGPT free tier supports writing tasks. Claude.ai has a free tier. Notion AI and Jasper are writing assistants. Writesonic and Copy.ai generate marketing content.

question: tool for building knowledge bases with AI
ground_truth: Notion AI and Confluence with AI plugins manage knowledge bases. Quivr and Khoj enable AI-powered personal knowledge bases. Obsidian with AI plugins supports local knowledge management.

question: AI infrastructure for running models at scale
ground_truth: Modal and Replicate host and scale ML models. BentoML packages models for deployment. Ray Serve handles distributed model serving. AWS SageMaker and Google Vertex AI are cloud options.

question: open source speech to text model
ground_truth: Whisper by OpenAI is the leading open-source speech-to-text model. FasterWhisper is an optimized Whisper implementation. Deepgram and AssemblyAI offer hosted speech-to-text APIs.

question: AI tool for analysing and querying databases
ground_truth: Text-to-SQL tools like Vanna.ai query databases with natural language. LangChain SQL agent connects LLMs to databases. MindsDB adds AI to databases directly. SQLCoder is an open-source text-to-SQL model.

question: low latency LLM inference API
ground_truth: Groq provides extremely fast LLM inference using LPU hardware. Together AI and Fireworks AI offer fast inference. Cerebras also provides high-speed LLM inference APIs.

question: AI tool for web scraping
ground_truth: Firecrawl scrapes websites into LLM-ready markdown. Spider and Crawl4AI are open-source web scrapers for AI. Apify provides cloud-based web scraping with AI extraction.

question: tool for building AI pipelines with visual interface
ground_truth: Flowise and Langflow are visual drag-and-drop LLM pipeline builders. Rivet by Ironclad supports graph-based AI workflows. n8n supports visual automation with LLM nodes.
